steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a singular subject ('The extensor digitorum brevis') and a direct object ('responsibility for extending the middle toes'). The blank must be a finite main verb that agrees with that singular subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'bearing': a present participle — non-finite, cannot serve as a main verb. ✗
  • Choice B — 'to bear': an infinitive — non-finite, cannot serve as a main verb. ✗
  • Choice C — 'bears': third-person singular present tense — a finite verb that agrees with the singular subject. ✓
  • Choice D — 'having borne': a perfect participle — non-finite, cannot serve as a main verb. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
